name: Route through Alhama - circular from Chimeneas
name_original: Ruta por Alhama — circular desde Chimeneas
region_intro: Hiking around Chimeneas offers a diverse landscape characterized by gentle terrain and historical points of interest. The region features routes that often incorporate natural hot springs and structures like Roman bridges. Trails frequently include sections near water features and provide views of the surrounding countryside. This area provides accessible hiking opportunities for various skill levels. Best hiking trails around Chimeneas The most popular hiking route is Santa Fe Hot Springs Loop, a 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area's natural hot springs. Another top favourite among local hikers is Via Ferrata – Roman Bridge loop from Cortijo de Las Presas, a moderate 6.9 miles (11.1 km) path. This trail features historical elements and varied terrain. Local hikers also love the Hot Springs – Santa Fe Hot Springs loop from Hoya de los Frailes, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through areas with hot springs, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Hiking around Chimeneas is defined by gentle hills, hot springs, and historical structures. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate hikes. The routes in Chimeneas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Chimeneas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Chimeneas?
A: The hiking trails around Chimeneas are generally characterized by gentle hills and varied terrain. You'll often find routes that incorporate natural hot springs, historical structures like Roman bridges, and sections near water features, offering diverse scenery.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Chimeneas?
A: Yes, Chimeneas offers a good selection of easy hikes. Out of the 47 available routes, 19 are classified as easy. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38259037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge – Via Ferrata loop from Cortijo de Las Presas&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 3.8-mile (6.1 km) path featuring historical elements.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Chimeneas area?
A: Many of the routes in Chimeneas are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e978080129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Hot Springs Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.9-mile (14.3 km) circular trail that explores the area's natural hot springs.

Q: What are some interesting historical or natural landmarks to see on the trails?
A: The region is rich in both natural and historical points of interest. You can explore natural hot springs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5511812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt;, or discover historical elements like Roman bridges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31537102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Ferrata – Roman Bridge loop from Cortijo de Las Presas&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to experience historical features.

Q: Are there any trails that feature hot springs?
A: Yes, hot springs are a prominent feature of the Chimeneas hiking experience. Several routes are designed around these natural wonders, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31530305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hot Springs – Santa Fe Hot Springs loop from Hoya de los Frailes&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you directly through areas with hot springs.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Chimeneas?
A: The trails in Chimeneas are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscape, the presence of natural hot springs, and the historical points of interest found along the routes.

Q: How long do hikes in Chimeneas typically take?
A: Hiking durations vary depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, a moderate trail like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e978080129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Hot Springs Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.9 miles (14.3 km), typically takes around 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. Shorter, easy routes can be completed in under 2 hours.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in Chimeneas offer scenic views of the surrounding countryside, characterized by gentle hills and agricultural landscapes. For expansive vistas, look for routes that pass through elevated areas or specific viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3303775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great agricultural landscape&lt;/a&gt; highlight.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Chimeneas?
A: Given the region's characteristics, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions in Chimeneas, with milder temperatures. However, the gentle terrain makes many trails accessible year-round, though summer can be hot and winter might bring cooler, potentially wetter, weather.

Q: Are there any places to explore near the trails, like reservoirs or historic towns?
A: Yes, the area around Chimeneas offers several points of interest. You can find large bodies of water such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/239989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Bermejales Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which is a popular spot. Additionally, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17238221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Historic Center – Santa Fe loop from Santa Fe&lt;/a&gt; allow you to explore nearby historic areas.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Chimeneas area?
A: There are over 45 hiking routes available for exploration around Chimeneas, catering to various skill levels. This includes 19 easy trails, 27 moderate routes, and a few more challenging options.

Q: Are there any specific cultural or historical sites to look out for?
A: Beyond Roman bridges, the region features other historical elements. For example, you might encounter structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4392542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aljibe Miñarro&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient cistern, which adds a cultural dimension to your hike.
tips: The aqueduct, built in the 20th century, is part of a complex network of canals and ditches that carry water to all irrigated areas.
Abandoned town very close to Castillo de Tajarja, very easy to reach. We can go through the few streets that it has and see part of the facades and interiors of the old houses.
